name: Dogfood Framework Audit + Delta (PR-F4, handover F-014)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Feature 044 is the SECOND dogfood feature: it (a) runs the SAME shipped tools/ecosystem_preflight.py (features 041/043, unmodified) a SECOND time against the framework's own tree under the SAME two-step operator-gate procedure contract 043 established, landing a SECOND .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id-2>/{preflight.json,baseline.json}; (b) conducts the framework's FIRST engineering audit (ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md) against that second baseline, landing .agent/audits/<audit-id>/findings.json (>=1 schema-valid finding) and .agent/audits/<audit-id>/report.md; (c) runs the framework's FIRST progress comparison (ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md) between the first baseline (043, execution-id dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253, revision e73cd04bad78c696c815bf253fb627a93f20c9c0) and the second, landing .agent/audits/<audit-id>/delta.json; and (d) updates docs/planning/ROADMAP.md from that evidence. It carries the SAME operator-gate exposure as 043 (feature_list_005.json marks 044's own operator_gate: false, but the UNDERLYING preflight mechanism's gate H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION still fires whenever the second run's own verdict is PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS -- expected here too, since this feature's own in-flight contract/qa artifacts and evidence-output directory recur as the same self-reference friction class NDEBT-018 already catalogues). This contract authorizes the two-step gated procedure a second time and the audit/delta/roadmap artifacts; it does NOT authorize the generator to fabricate or self-supply operator approval, and it does NOT authorize fixing any catalogued debt (NDEBT-015/016/017 remain open; this feature classifies and cites them, it does not remediate them).
design_notes.ordering_invariant: Per ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 2 ('An audit MUST NOT begin until a clean-state preflight run has returned a PASS verdict, or a PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS verdict an operator has explicitly approved... and a baseline has been captured') and ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md Sec 2 ('Both inputs MUST already be captured and approved before the comparison begins'), this feature's implementation steps run in this STRICT order: (1) the second two-step preflight+baseline gate (mirroring 043) completes in full -- pending step, human relay, approved step, move-semantics hand-off into .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id-2>/ -- BEFORE any audit artifact is authored; (2) ONLY once baseline #2 is captured and approved does the audit (findings.json, evidence files) get authored against it; (3) ONLY once both baselines are captured/approved and the audit findings exist does the comparison (delta.json) get authored; (4) report.md and docs/planning/ROADMAP.md are authored last, from the now-complete evidence. This ordering also has a load-bearing side effect: because .agent/audits/ does not exist yet when the second preflight run's tolerate-list is resolved, it never needs to appear in that tolerate-list -- the bounded set for THIS feature's own preflight run is structurally identical in kind to 043's (pre-existing friction file + this feature's own in-flight contract/qa artifacts + its own evidence-output directory), not larger.
design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure_second_run: IDENTICAL mechanics to contract 043's two_step_gate_procedure, reapplied for a NEW execution-id (dry-run-proven in this contract's own verification entries against a fresh disposable clone at real HEAD 6d7a47b): (1) PENDING STEP -- resolve the bounded tolerate-list, invoke `python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --execution-id <execution-id-2> --output-dir .agent/evidence/<execution-id-2>/pending --self-fixture <resolved --tolerate-untracked flags>` with NO operator-approval flags; expected exit 2, writes ONLY preflight.pending.json + collection-log.txt. The generator MUST STOP and relay the pending exceptions to the orchestrator for a REAL human decision under gate H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION -- it must not invent an approval. (2) APPROVED STEP -- ONLY after a real, recorded operator decision is relayed, re-invoke the SAME CLI with the tolerate-list RECOMPUTED FRESH (the pending step's own output directory is now itself untracked) PLUS --operator-approver/--operator-authorization carrying the REAL relayed values, --output-dir .../approved; expected exit 3, writes preflight.json + baseline.json + collection-log.txt. The generator then MOVES (never copies -- see 043's no_evidence_duplication_of_verdict_artifacts, which this feature inherits verbatim) exactly the two schema-gated files into .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id-2>/, leaving only collection-log.txt behind in approved/. (3) The orchestrator's own gate-decision write to .agent/run_state.json (an orchestrator-owned field this contract never edits) MUST be committed BEFORE the approved-step invocation runs, per NDEBT-018 lesson (2) -- discovered for real during 043's own implementation, where the uncommitted run_state.json write caused a genuine exit-1 FAIL on the first approved-step attempt. This feature's generator dispatch must relay this ordering requirement to the orchestrator exactly as 043's did.
design_notes.execution_id_2_and_audit_id_conventions: execution-id-2 (the second dogfood preflight+baseline run) reuses 043's PINNED FORMULA verbatim, applied a second time: `dogfood-<UTC-date:YYYY-MM-DD>-<7-char-short-SHA-of-HEAD-at-pending-step-time>`. At contract-authoring time, real HEAD is 6d7a47b, so a dry run computes `dogfood-2026-07-17-6d7a47b` -- verified in this contract's own verification entries to collide with nothing already present under .agent/reconciliation/ or .agent/evidence/ (the only existing dogfood-* id is dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253, a different SHA). Per the NDEBT-018 lesson (2) above, the orchestrator's gate-decision commit will likely advance HEAD again between the pending and approved steps (exactly as it did for 043, 28c8253 -> e73cd04), so the REAL execution-id-2's SHA suffix is recomputed once at the start of the pending step and reused verbatim through the approved step, per 043's own execution_id_convention -- it is NOT required to still equal 6d7a47b by the time implementation actually runs. audit-id (the audit+delta+report artifact set) is a DISTINCT, disjoint-prefix convention -- never `dogfood-`, mirroring 043's evidence_vs_reconciliation_split precedent -- pinned as `audit-<UTC-date:YYYY-MM-DD>-<7-char-short-SHA-of-HEAD-at-audit-authoring-time>`. Since no commit happens between the approved preflight step and the audit-authoring step within one feature implementation pass (the established single-commit-per-feature precedent, e.g. commit c8a1e25 for feature 042), audit-id's date+SHA suffix will equal execution-id-2's own suffix; the `audit-` vs `dogfood-` prefix keeps the two artifact families' paths disjoint and never colliding on a shared filename.
design_notes.finding_sourcing_and_confidence_discipli: REVISED at revision 1 per .agent/qa/044-contract-review.json HIGH findings 1 and 2 -- see approvals.round0_disposition for the full rejection/fix record. Per ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 3/5, every finding's confidence is capped by its evidence tier, and no finding may be recorded at a confidence higher than its evidence supports. This contract sources exactly five REAL findings from the live DEBT register and this run's own dogfood evidence -- none fabricated, none hypothetical -- each with a PINNED, EXACT id (no longer an informal 'F1'..'F5' label): (id `F-audit044-ndebt-015`) NDEBT-015 (`tools/validate.sh --target` misroutes all three ecosystem fixture families) -- status open, confidence EXACTLY Confirmed. Evidence: a FRESH, real invocation of the shipped, unmodified validator (`bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_valid.json`) captured at implementation time, which is Tier 1 evidence per Sec 3 -- the mechanism deterministically and reproducibly demonstrates the MISROUTE itself (the same fixture always falls through to [C4] FAIL with the same 'framework is a required property' NIZAM-schema error), independently reproducible by anyone with the same tooling, which is exactly Confirmed's definition (Sec 5). (id `F-audit044-ndebt-002`) NDEBT-002 (schema/qa_verdict.schema.json required-key mismatch; nothing validated .agent/qa/*.json) -- status RESOLVED, confidence EXACTLY Confirmed. closure_evidence cites the two REAL, git-verifiable commits DEBT.md's own Resolved section already names -- bcb02d2 ('feat(004): reconcile schemas to actual artifact shapes -- R4a (feature 025)') and 0840094 ('feat(004): C11 dogfood schema validation of .agent/ -- R4b (feature 028)'). CORRECTED FRAMING (fix for HIGH finding 2): this finding resolved on 2026-07-10, well BEFORE baseline #1 was ever captured (2026-07-17) -- confirmed via `git show e73cd04:docs/planning/DEBT.md`, which already lists it under `## Resolved` at baseline #1's own pinned revision. Per ecosystem/07 Sec 3, delta.json's `resolved` field is a CROSS-EXECUTION TRANSITION class ('present in the earlier execution, absent from the later one') -- nothing about NDEBT-002 transitioned in the window this delta measures, so it is NEVER placed in delta.json's `findings.resolved` transition bucket (which is honestly EMPTY, see design_notes.resolved_class_is_pre_baseline1_only below). It IS recorded, correctly, as a status:'resolved' entry in findings.json (satisfying the schema's closure_evidence requirement and the closure-only-with-evidence rule with a genuine, already-closed, non-contrived example), and report.md / the ROADMAP.md bullet are REQUIRED (new verification entries) to carry the exact literal phrase 'previously resolved (pre-baseline-1)' adjacent to its mention, so no reader can mistake it for an in-window closure. Its 'unchanged, stays-resolved' status across both baseline-1 and baseline-2 supports the unchanged_facts demonstration instead (design_notes.unchanged_and_changed_baseline_facts). (id `F-audit044-ndebt-016`) NDEBT-016 (C12 fixture-polarity regex has confirmed dodges for FUTURE fixture filenames) -- status open, confidence EXACTLY Suspected (Tier 3/4: disclosed only in contract 042's own design_notes prose, dated 2026-07-17, never independently re-run or re-confirmed by anyone since). Per HIGH finding 1, this confidence value is now a PINNED, individually-checked assertion (verification entry 24) -- a silent upgrade to 'Confirmed' via a freshly-stamped timestamp, without genuinely re-running the underlying regex check, is structurally caught, not merely hoped against. This audit does NOT re-verify it -- deliberately, per the stale-evidence-non-reuse rule (ecosystem/07 Sec 5) -- so its evidence genuinely predates baseline #2's own captured_at and is flagged `stale` in delta.json (the ONLY id permitted in the stale set, per entry 16's exact-membership assertion). (id `F-audit044-ndebt-017`) NDEBT-017 (ci_references honest KNOWN LIMITATION, first exercised for real by 043) -- status open, confidence EXACTLY Confirmed. Evidence: baseline #2's OWN freshly-captured ci_references field. Absent from DEBT.md at revision e73cd04 (baseline #1's pinned revision -- confirmed empirically: `git show e73cd04:docs/planning/DEBT.md` names no NDEBT-017), present at baseline #2's revision -- ONE of exactly two ids permitted in delta.json's `new` set (entry 16's exact-membership assertion). (id `F-audit044-ndebt-018`) NDEBT-018 (dogfood preflight self-reference) -- status open, confidence EXACTLY Confirmed. Evidence: THIS feature's own second preflight run's live pending/approved exception sets, which independently reproduce the SAME self-reference friction class DEBT.md's NDEBT-018 already catalogues -- also absent from DEBT.md at revision e73cd04, present at baseline #2's revision -- the OTHER of exactly two ids permitted in delta.json's `new` set. Summary of the exact per-id pins verification entries 16/24 now enforce: delta `new` == {`F-audit044-ndebt-017`, `F-audit044-ndebt-018`} (exactly these two, nothing more/less); delta `stale` == {`F-audit044-ndebt-016`} (exactly this one); delta `resolved` == {} (honestly empty, see design_notes.resolved_class_is_pre_baseline1_only); delta `reopened` == {} (honestly empty, see design_notes.reopened_class_is_mechanism_only); findings.json confidence pins: `F-audit044-ndebt-016` == exactly 'Suspected', all four others == exactly 'Confirmed'; findings.json status pins: `F-audit044-ndebt-002` == exactly 'resolved' with non-empty closure_evidence, all four others == exactly 'open'.
design_notes.resolved_class_is_pre_baseline1_only: NEW at revision 1, per .agent/qa/044-contract-review.json HIGH finding 2 (Observation B) -- the reviewer's recommended fix, applied cleanly rather than adding a disclosure-phrase escape hatch: delta.json's `resolved` TRANSITION class (ecosystem/07 Sec 3) means 'present in the earlier execution, absent from the later one' -- a genuine in-window closure. NDEBT-002 (`F-audit044-ndebt-002`) is the only resolved-status finding this contract sources, and it was ALREADY resolved before baseline #1 ever existed (git-history-proven: present in `## Resolved` at revision e73cd04). Placing it in delta.json's `findings.resolved` transition bucket would therefore be a category error -- nothing transitioned in this comparison's own window. This contract's real delta.json's `resolved` array is therefore honestly EMPTY, mirroring exactly the same honesty discipline design_notes.reopened_class_is_mechanism_only already applies to `reopened` -- this is the framework's FIRST-EVER comparison, so there is no in-window resolution to report any more than there is a prior comparison for anything to reopen against; an empty array is the correct, evidence-first answer to 'did anything resolve in this window', not a gap. The closure-only-with-evidence RULE (ecosystem/07 Sec 4) is still demonstrated with a REAL example -- just at the findings.json level (a finding CAN be, and is, recorded status:'resolved' with real closure_evidence there), never conflated with the delta's cross-execution transition semantics. report.md and the ROADMAP.md bullet are both REQUIRED (new, dedicated verification entries 17/18) to carry the exact literal phrase 'previously resolved (pre-baseline-1)' immediately adjacent to NDEBT-002's mention, so the distinction is legible to a human reader of the tracked, human-facing ROADMAP.md, not just enforced internally in delta.json's structure.
design_notes.reopened_class_is_mechanism_only: Per ecosystem/07 Sec 3, `reopened` means 'previously classified resolved in an earlier comparison, now present again in the later execution's findings.' This framework has never run a comparison before (this feature IS the first), so there is no earlier comparison's resolved-classification for anything to reopen against -- a real reopened instance does not yet exist, and this contract does NOT fabricate one. The real delta.json's `reopened` array is therefore honestly EMPTY -- exactly the same honesty discipline design_notes.resolved_class_is_pre_baseline1_only applies to `resolved` above, for the analogous 'first-ever comparison' reason. The classification LOGIC's ability to recognize a reopened finding when one eventually occurs is proven ONLY as a mechanism dry-run in a disposable, clearly-labeled fixture (never presented as a real production instance) -- this is an explicit, disclosed limitation of THIS run, not a defect: an empty array is the honest answer to 'has anything reopened yet', not a gap to be papered over with a manufactured example.
design_notes.unchanged_and_changed_baseline_facts: The product_spec_005 Sec 9 requirement 'unchanged facts remain stable' / 'a deliberately changed fixture is detected' is proven at the BASELINE FIELD level (ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 3's six categories), distinct from the FOUR FINDING-transition classes above: delta.json's `unchanged_facts` array cites baseline #1 vs baseline #2's `dependency_references[0].revision` (the python3 runtime version) -- dry-run-proven identical (3.14.4) in a fresh disposable clone at real HEAD, matching baseline #1's own recorded 3.14.4 -- and NDEBT-002's continued presence in DEBT.md's Resolved section, unchanged, at both e73cd04 and the current tree (dry-run-proven: `git show e73cd04:docs/planning/DEBT.md` and the current file both name NDEBT-002 exactly once, in the Resolved section) -- this is where NDEBT-002 (`F-audit044-ndebt-002`) is represented at the DELTA level: purely as an unchanged baseline fact, NEVER in the `findings.resolved` transition array (see design_notes.resolved_class_is_pre_baseline1_only). `unchanged_facts` array entries are content-checked (verification entry 27, new at revision 1) to cite the ACTUAL revision/value strings, not merely assert the key exists. delta.json's `changed_facts` array cites `repository_references[0].revision` (and `framework_references[0].revision`) -- baseline #1 pins e73cd04bad78c696c815bf253fb627a93f20c9c0, baseline #2 pins whatever HEAD is at its own real approved-step invocation (necessarily a later commit than e73cd04, since features and their commits have continued to land) -- a REAL, honestly-detected change, never a fabricated one; the passage of real framework commits between the two dogfood executions IS the deliberate change this feature detects, exactly as product_spec_005 Sec 9 anticipates.
design_notes.revision_field_not_execution_id_label_in: MANDATORY, per the Mode-B N1 lesson from contract 043's own real artifact: 043's execution-id is `dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253` (the SHA of the PENDING step's HEAD), but its baseline.json's own `repository_references[0].revision` field pins `e73cd04bad78c696c815bf253fb627a93f20c9c0` (a DIFFERENT, LATER commit -- the orchestrator's gate-decision commit that had to land before the approved step could succeed, per NDEBT-018 lesson (2)). A naive comparison mechanism that extracted the trailing hex characters from an execution-id STRING instead of reading the baseline artifact's own `revision` FIELD would silently compare the WRONG commit (28c8253, which was never actually baselined) against whatever baseline #2 pins, corrupting every fact in unchanged_and_changed_baseline_facts above. This contract pins, as a binding invariant with its own dedicated verification entry: every revision cited anywhere in delta.json (baseline-before, baseline-after, or any finding's evidence revision) is read from the corresponding baseline.json/findings.json artifact's own declared `revision` field, NEVER derived by string-parsing an execution-id or audit-id label. Dry-run-proven against the REAL, already-committed baseline #1 artifact: its execution-id's trailing 7 hex characters (28c8253) do NOT equal, and are not a prefix of, its own baseline.json's real revision field (e73cd04...).
design_notes.roadmap_bounded_shape: Per the NDEBT-014 catalogue item 5 and contract 040's own three-round circuit-breaker history ('natural-language content bounds... cannot stop paraphrase mirroring -- only closed-set shapes with exact-text pinning proved airtight'), the ROADMAP.md addition is PINNED to a bounded, closed shape rather than open free prose: exactly one new dated subsection under the EXACT, literal, verification-pinned heading `## Dogfood Audit + Delta (` (entry 18 greps for this exact literal, per HIGH finding 3's anti-vacuity fix), containing exactly: (a) one sentence naming the audit-id and both compared execution-ids/revisions, (b) a fixed-shape bullet list of the four transition-class counts with their concrete finding IDs -- new: 2 (`F-audit044-ndebt-017`, `F-audit044-ndebt-018`), resolved: 0 (honestly empty, see design_notes.resolved_class_is_pre_baseline1_only), reopened: 0 (honestly empty), stale: 1 (`F-audit044-ndebt-016`) -- (c) the exact literal phrase 'previously resolved (pre-baseline-1)' immediately adjacent to NDEBT-002's own mention (entry 18's second required grep, per HIGH finding 2's fix), and (d) one sentence naming the debt items the roadmap's own forward-looking scope should reprioritize given this audit's evidence (citing NDEBT-015 as the highest-value next self-compliance candidate, corroborating Track 2 item 1's existing NDEBT-007 pairing rather than contradicting it). No other existing ROADMAP.md line is touched (frontmatter version bump + change_log entry only, per the house convention identical commits 17408d9/28c8253/e73cd04 already establish for DEBT.md); the addition is measured (added_lines >=1 and <=25, BOTH bounds now genuinely load-bearing per HIGH finding 3's grep -E fix, re-proven against real 0/12/26-line probes), capped, and exact-text-verifiable, not an unbounded narrative the generator could silently pad or drift.
design_notes.estimated_lines_derivation: Measured from the disposable-clone dry run of the second preflight gate (identical shape to 043's own measured artifacts) plus the audit/delta/report additions: .agent/reconciliation/<id-2>/preflight.json ~18 lines, baseline.json ~47 lines, .agent/evidence/<id-2>/{pending,approved}/{preflight.pending.json,collection-log.txt} ~35 lines combined, .agent/audits/<audit-id>/findings.json ~90 lines (5 findings x ~15-20 lines each incl. evidence arrays), .agent/audits/<audit-id>/delta.json ~55 lines, .agent/audits/<audit-id>/report.md ~40 lines (human-readable narrative), a handful of small raw-evidence files under .agent/evidence/<audit-id>/ ~20 lines combined, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md +12 lines (the bounded subsection + frontmatter bump). Total estimate ~320 lines -- over feature_list_005.json's a-priori 150-line estimate, reflecting that this feature does TWO first-time things at once (a second gated preflight run AND the framework's first-ever audit + delta), each individually comparable in size to 043's own ~175-line actual. Flagged here for orchestrator scope-budget awareness at contract-approval time, consistent with the Scope Budget Protocol (flag, not halt, pending the per-feature 3x-rolling-average check the orchestrator computes against 042/043's actuals at approval time).
design_notes.scope_budget_context: Per .agent/run_state.json scope_budget (original_estimate_lines 3500 post-rebaseline, 130% ceiling 4550), cumulative through feature 043 = 3184 lines = 91% of the 3500 base / 70% of the 4550 ceiling. This contract's ~320-line estimate would bring cumulative to ~3504 (100.1% of the 3500 base, 77% of the 4550 ceiling) -- no cumulative-ceiling breach. Per-feature check: rolling average of the last 3 features (041=501, 042=141, 043=175) = 272.3, 3x = 817; 320 is well under, so no per-feature scope flag is expected, but the orchestrator makes that determination against the REAL implementation actuals, not this a-priori estimate.

'framework' is a required property` -- proving F1's Confirmed-tier evidence claim; (7) `git cat-file -t bcb02d2` / `0840094` / `758ded4` on the REAL repository confirm all three cited closure/resolution commits are real, existing commit objects; (8) `git show e73cd04:docs/planning/DEBT.md` on the REAL repository's own tracked history confirms NDEBT-017 and NDEBT-018 are ABSENT at baseline #1's pinned revision while present at the current tree, and confirms NDEBT-002 and NDEBT-016 are ALREADY present (in Resolved / Open sections respectively) at that same revision, exactly as design_notes above requires; (9) the shipped negative fixture tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_neg_resolved_without_closure_evidence.json correctly FAILS schema validation against schema/engineering_finding.schema.json (missing closure_evidence), proving the closure-only-with-evidence rule is already structurally enforced with no new fixture authoring required; (10) the execution-id-2 formula, computed against the real repository's real current HEAD, collides with nothing already present under .agent/reconciliation/ or .agent/evidence/. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these proofs, other than this contract file itself; every operator-approver/-authorization value used in any proof was the clearly-labeled sentinel string 'dry-run-mechanism-proof' inside a disposable fixture only. REVISION 1 additions, per .agent/qa/044-contract-review.json (all re-proven in the evaluator's own scratchpad probes, never touching the real repository tree): (11) a deliberately MISCLASSIFIED delta.json fixture (NDEBT-002 placed in `new`, NDEBT-017 placed in `resolved` with zero closure evidence, NDEBT-018 placed in `stale`) is run through the REVISED entry 16's exact per-id-membership assertions and correctly FAILS (was: incorrectly PASSED under the rev0 entry 16, the exact HIGH finding 1 defect); (12) a fixture findings.json silently upgrading NDEBT-016's confidence from 'Suspected' to 'Confirmed' correctly FAILS the revised entry 24's exact per-id confidence pin; (13) a disposable ROADMAP.md probe with a REAL 26-line addition correctly FAILS the revised entry 18 (added_lines=26 > 25), a REAL 12-line addition correctly PASSES (added_lines=12, within 1-25), and a REAL 0-line no-op (unmodified ROADMAP.md) correctly FAILS the new anti-vacuity floor (added_lines=0, not >=1) -- all three re-run with the corrected `grep -v -E '^\+\+\+'` (the rev0 defect was the missing `-E`, which let GNU grep's BRE `\+` repetition-operator extension misfire and silently zero every added-lines count regardless of actual content); (14) the shipped, already-committed contract 043's own real artifact `.agent/reconciliation/dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253/preflight.json` is read directly to confirm its real, non-sentinel operator_approval (`approver: rosscn@nizamiq.com`) is exactly the shape the new operator-approval-sentinel entry (26) requires for the SECOND run's own real artifact, confirming the check's positive case is achievable and not merely a fixture-only proof.

"*) path="${line:3}"; case "$path" in .agent/contracts/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/contracts/04[0-4]*.json|.agent/qa/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/qa/04[0-4]*.json|.agent/reconciliation/dogfood-*|.agent/evidence/dogfood-*|.agent/evidence/043-verify-*.txt|.agent/evidence/043/*|.agent/evidence/044-verify-*.txt|.agent/evidence/044/*|.agent/audits/audit-*|.agent/evidence/audit-*) ;; *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent artifact: $path"; bad=1 ;; esac ;; esac; done < <(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts .agent/reconciliation .agent/evidence .agent/audits); if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: GATE-DEPENDENT (post-implementation, REVISED at revision 1 per HIGH finding 1): the real .agent/audits/<audit-id>/findings.json contains 5 entries all valid against schema/engineering_finding.schema.json (F-014's literal acceptance test), and EXACT per-id confidence is pinned -- F-audit044-ndebt-016 == exactly 'Suspected' (so a silent upgrade cannot go undetected), the other four == exactly 'Confirmed' -- and EXACT per-id status is pinned -- F-audit044-ndebt-002 == 'resolved' with non-empty closure_evidence, the other four == 'open'.
verification.command:
AUDIT_DIR=$(ls -dt .agent/audits/audit-* 2>/dev/null | head -1); [ -n "$AUDIT_DIR" ] || { echo 'no .agent/audits/audit-* directory found yet'; exit 1; }; python3 -c "import json, jsonschema
EXPECTED_CONFIDENCE = {'F-audit044-ndebt-015': 'Confirmed', 'F-audit044-ndebt-002': 'Confirmed', 'F-audit044-ndebt-016': 'Suspected', 'F-audit044-ndebt-017': 'Confirmed', 'F-audit044-ndebt-018': 'Confirmed'}
s = json.load(open('schema/engineering_finding.schema.json'))
findings = json.load(open('${AUDIT_DIR}/findings.json'))
assert isinstance(findings, list) and len(findings) >= 1
for f in findings:
    jsonschema.validate(f, s)
by_id = {f['id']: f for f in findings}
for fid, expected_conf in EXPECTED_CONFIDENCE.items():
    assert fid in by_id, f'missing expected finding id {fid}'
    actual = by_id[fid]['confidence']
    assert actual == expected_conf, f'{fid} confidence must be EXACTLY {expected_conf!r}, got {actual!r}'
assert by_id['F-audit044-ndebt-002']['status'] == 'resolved', 'F-audit044-ndebt-002 must be status resolved'
assert by_id['F-audit044-ndebt-002'].get('closure_evidence'), 'F-audit044-ndebt-002 must carry non-empty closure_evidence'
for fid in ('F-audit044-ndebt-015','F-audit044-ndebt-016','F-audit044-ndebt-017','F-audit044-ndebt-018'):
    actual_status = by_id[fid]['status']
    assert actual_status == 'open', f'{fid} must be status open, got {actual_status}'
print('PASS')
" && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: GATE-DEPENDENT (post-implementation, NEW at revision 1 -- missing_acceptance_coverage item 2): delta.json's unchanged_facts/changed_facts arrays cite the ACTUAL revision/value strings from the two real baseline.json files (not merely the right keys) -- dependency_references identical and cited in unchanged_facts; repository_references differing and both values cited in changed_facts; compared_baselines.before/after.revision equal the real baseline fields exactly.
verification.command:
EXEC_ID2=$(basename $(ls -dt .agent/reconciliation/dogfood-* 2>/dev/null | grep -v 'dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253' | head -1) 2>/dev/null); AUDIT_DIR=$(ls -dt .agent/audits/audit-* 2>/dev/null | head -1); [ -n "$EXEC_ID2" ] && [ -n "$AUDIT_DIR" ] || { echo 'second baseline or audit directory not found yet'; exit 1; }; python3 -c "import json
b1 = json.load(open('.agent/reconciliation/dogfood-2026-07-17-28c8253/baseline.json'))
b2 = json.load(open('.agent/reconciliation/${EXEC_ID2}/baseline.json'))
d = json.load(open('${AUDIT_DIR}/delta.json'))
rev1 = b1['repository_references'][0]['revision']
rev2 = b2['repository_references'][0]['revision']
dep1 = b1['dependency_references'][0]['revision']
dep2 = b2['dependency_references'][0]['revision']
assert dep1 == dep2, ('dependency_references expected stable', dep1, dep2)
assert rev1 != rev2, ('repository_references expected to differ (deliberate change)', rev1, rev2)
unchanged_blob = json.dumps(d['unchanged_facts'])
changed_blob = json.dumps(d['changed_facts'])
assert dep1 in unchanged_blob, 'unchanged_facts must cite the ACTUAL stable dependency revision value'
assert rev1 in changed_blob and rev2 in changed_blob, 'changed_facts must cite the ACTUAL before/after revision values'
cb = d['compared_baselines']
assert cb['before']['revision'] == rev1, ('compared_baselines.before.revision must equal the real baseline #1 field', cb['before'].get('revision'), rev1)
assert cb['after']['revision'] == rev2, ('compared_baselines.after.revision must equal the real baseline #2 field', cb['after'].get('revision'), rev2)
print('PASS')
" && echo PASS || exit 1

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/044-verify-NN.txt (the pre-existing FLAT per-contract convention, methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5 -- distinct from, and never colliding with, the second dogfood run's own .agent/evidence/<execution-id-2>/{pending,approved}/ convention and the audit's own .agent/evidence/<audit-id>/ raw-finding-evidence convention; see design_notes.execution_id_2_and_audit_id_conventions). NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (the 'verification' array has exactly 27 entries, so .agent/evidence/044-verify-01.txt through .agent/evidence/044-verify-27.txt). Every evidence file MUST comply with the Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths.

approvals.round0_disposition: REJECTED at revision 0 by the evaluator (three HIGH findings, two missing_acceptance_coverage items; Mode A's carried-forward advisories reproduced and mostly concurred non-blocking, except A1's mitigation being fully ABSENT here, escalated to coverage item 1). HIGH 1 (verification entry 16, extended to entry 24): no per-finding-id correctness -- a fixture delta with NDEBT-002 fabricated into 'new', NDEBT-017 into 'resolved' with zero closure evidence, and NDEBT-018 into 'stale' passed the rev0 check (only top-level-key presence and non-empty-length were asserted). FIX (this revision): entry 16 now asserts EXACT set membership per transition class by finding id (new == exactly {F-audit044-ndebt-017, F-audit044-ndebt-018}; stale == exactly {F-audit044-ndebt-016}; resolved == exactly [] per HIGH 2's fix below), asserts closure_evidence structurally for anything ever in resolved, and entry 24 now pins F-audit044-ndebt-016's confidence to exactly 'Suspected' (and all four others to exactly 'Confirmed') so a timestamp refresh cannot silently game the stale classification. HIGH 2 (NDEBT-002 framing, category error under ecosystem/07 Sec 3): NDEBT-002 resolved on 2026-07-10, well BEFORE baseline #1 (2026-07-17) -- git-history-proven present in DEBT.md's Resolved section already at revision e73cd04 -- so placing it in delta.json's cross-execution `resolved` TRANSITION bucket was a category error (nothing actually transitioned in this window). FIX (this revision, the reviewer's recommended option): NDEBT-002 is REMOVED from delta.json's `resolved` transition class entirely -- that array is now honestly EMPTY (first-comparison bootstrap: no in-window resolution exists, mirroring the already-honest empty `reopened[]`) -- while findings.json still carries it as a status:'resolved' finding WITH real closure_evidence (satisfying the schema + Sec 4's closure rule with a genuine, already-closed example). A new design_notes key (resolved_class_is_pre_baseline1_only) documents the decision; new verification-entry requirements pin the exact literal phrase 'previously resolved (pre-baseline-1)' adjacent to NDEBT-002's mention in BOTH report.md (entry 17) and the tracked, human-facing ROADMAP.md (entry 18), so no reader mistakes it for an in-window closure. HIGH 3 (verification entry 18, grep BRE bug): the second grep in the added-lines pipeline (`grep -v '^\+\+\+'`) was missing `-E`, so GNU grep's BRE `\+` repetition-operator extension misfired and structurally zeroed added_lines regardless of actual content -- 0/12/26-line additions all reported added_lines=0/PASS. FIX (this revision): `grep -v -E '^\+\+\+'` (matching the sibling clause's own -E usage), plus an anti-vacuity floor (added_lines must be >=1, so a no-op ROADMAP.md 'update' now correctly fails a GATE-DEPENDENT check), plus a literal-heading presence check, re-proven against real 0/12/26-line disposable-clone probes (0 and 26 now correctly FAIL, 12 correctly PASSES). COVERAGE 1 (missing_acceptance_coverage item 1): added a dedicated GATE-DEPENDENT operator-approval-sentinel-check entry for the SECOND run's real preflight.json, mirroring contract 043's own precedent entry, closing the real coverage regression the reviewer identified (043 had it, 044 rev0 dropped it with no disclosed reason). COVERAGE 2 (missing_acceptance_coverage item 2): added a dedicated GATE-DEPENDENT entry asserting delta.json's unchanged_facts/changed_facts arrays cite the ACTUAL revision/value strings from the two real baseline.json files, not merely the right top-level keys. Verification entries grew from 25 to 27 (two new: the operator-approval sentinel check and the delta content-correctness check); entries 16/17/18/24 were revised in place (same indices, stronger assertions). All entries re-run verbatim (extracted via json.load, never transcribed) against a fresh disposable `cp -a` clone of real HEAD 6d7a47b6652d62a03d58dec01fe320ee0075a559 plus non-mutating real-repo reads: the 19 mechanism/structural entries PASS as written; the 8 GATE-DEPENDENT entries (16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27) correctly and expectedly FAIL pre-implementation (anti-vacuity pattern) -- EXCEPT that 18's fixed anti-vacuity floor now ALSO correctly fails on a genuinely unmodified ROADMAP.md, closing the rev0 vacuity gap the reviewer separately noted. The evaluator's three named kill-shot fixtures were re-run against the revised checks in a disposable scratchpad (never touching the real repository tree): (a) the deliberately misclassified delta.json fixture (NDEBT-002 in new, NDEBT-017 in resolved with no closure evidence, NDEBT-018 in stale) now correctly FAILS entry 16; (b) a disposable ROADMAP.md probe with a real 26-line addition now correctly FAILS entry 18 (added_lines=26 > 25); (c) the same probe with a real 12-line addition correctly PASSES entry 18 (added_lines=12, within 1-25). Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these revision proofs, other than this contract file itself. status remains 'proposed'; scope.files_create/files_modify are otherwise unchanged; estimated_lines (320) is unchanged, since these are contract-verification-array and design_notes clarifications, not feature-deliverable growth.
approvals.round1_review_ref: .agent/qa/044-contract-review-rev1.json (evaluator's round-2 approval of revision 1's substantive content, final_verdict.approved=true, with one non-blocking bookkeeping nit -- the same 7-vs-8 gate-dependent-count undercount Mode A also flagged); Mode A's SEPARATE, blocking rejection (the entry-24 quoting/crash defect) was relayed via the orchestrator's dispatch message only -- no distinct .agent/qa/*.json artifact was provided for that specific Mode A pass
approvals.round1_disposition: Round 2 of 3. Evaluator APPROVED revision 1's substantive fixes (HIGH 1/2/3 and both coverage gaps from round0_review_ref all confirmed sound). Mode A REJECTED on ONE isolated blocker: verification entry 24's assert message -- 'F-audit044-ndebt-002 must be status resolved (quoted)' -- used a RAW, literal double-quote character inside the `python3 -c \"...\"` wrapper (the same escaped-double-quote delimiter convention every other python3 -c invocation in this contract uses). That literal `"` truncated the outer bash string early, and the residual, corrupted shell tokens happened to still parse as SOME python invocation, which then crashed with an unrelated `SyntaxError: leading zeros in decimal integer literals` -- on BOTH a correct 5-finding fixture and a deliberately-gamed one. Zero discriminating signal; the check was unsatisfiable at Mode B regardless of the real artifact's honesty. This is the SECOND quoting bug in this contract (the first, an analogous raw-`"`-inside-`-c \"...\"` collision in entry 16's resolved[]-emptiness message, was caught and fixed at rev1's own authoring time via `bash -n` syntax-checking every command -- but that same systematic sweep was NOT re-run after entry 24 was independently revised later in the same rev1 pass, and rev1's kill-shot proof only exercised entries 16 and 18 against real fixtures, never entry 24 -- an honest process gap this disposition records rather than papers over). Mode A additionally flagged two prose defects in round0_disposition, both now corrected in place (see the text above, not duplicated here): (a) round0_disposition's summary mischaracterized entry 24's pre-implementation failure as ordinary, benign anti-vacuity (the same class as entries 16-18/23/25-27's expected 'no artifact yet' failures) -- in fact entry 24 additionally carried the quoting defect above, which would have made it unsatisfiable even AFTER implementation; (b) round0_disposition underscounted the GATE-DEPENDENT entry list as 7 entries (16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 26, 27), omitting entry 25 -- corrected to the true count of 8 (16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27), with the mechanism/structural count corrected from 20 to 19 (27 total - 8 gate-dependent = 19) to match. FIX (this revision): entry 24's assert message rewritten as a single-quoted Python string literal with NO double-quote character anywhere in the -c body -- 'F-audit044-ndebt-002 must be status resolved' (dropped the decorative quotes around 'resolved' entirely, since a single-quoted Python string cannot itself contain an unescaped single quote either; this matches the plain, unquoted-value convention every other assert message in this contract already uses). VERIFICATION (this revision): (1) `bash -n -c "<cmd>"` syntax-checked against ALL 27 verification commands -- all 27 syntactically valid; (2) a dedicated regex sweep for ANY raw `"` character nested inside a `python3 -c \"...\"`-delimited body across all 27 commands -- zero hits; (3) entry 24 (extracted from the revised .agent/contracts/044.json via json.load, never transcribed) re-run verbatim against three disposable-scratchpad fixtures, never touching the real repository tree: (i) a correct 5-finding findings.json (all ids/confidences/statuses honest) -> PASS; (ii) the same set with F-audit044-ndebt-016's confidence dishonestly flipped from Suspected to Confirmed -> FAILS on the confidence assertion (F-audit044-ndebt-016 confidence must be EXACTLY Suspected, got Confirmed); (iii) the same set with F-audit044-ndebt-002's status dishonestly flipped from resolved to open -> FAILS on the (now-fixed) status assertion ('F-audit044-ndebt-002 must be status resolved') -- no crash, genuine discriminating signal restored on both directions. status remains 'proposed'; scope/non_goals/estimated_lines unchanged; this is a mechanical quoting fix plus a prose correction, not a design or scope change.
approvals.round2_disposition: Round 3 of 3 -- BOTH gates APPROVED. Mode A re-confirmed the entry-24 quoting fix directly: re-ran the exact revised command (via json.load, never transcribed) against three fixtures -- (i) a correct 5-finding set -> clean PASS; (ii) NDEBT-016 confidence gamed Suspected->Confirmed -> clean, specific AssertionError, no crash; (iii) NDEBT-002 status gamed resolved->open -> clean, specific AssertionError, no crash -- plus a zero-hazard sweep confirming no other entry carries the same nested-raw-double-quote defect class. The evaluator's round-3 delta confirmation (.agent/qa/044-contract-review-rev2.json) independently reproduced the ORIGINAL revision-1 crash first (re-running its own saved byte-identical copy of revision 1's entry 24 against both a correct and a confidence-gamed fixture -- both crashed identically with `SyntaxError: leading zeros in decimal integer literals`, exit 1, zero discriminating signal, exactly Mode A's diagnosis), then confirmed the fix closes it three ways against revision 2's entry 24 (the same three fixtures above, plus a fresh third fixture of its own construction), and byte-diffed all 26 unchanged verification commands (1-23, 25-27) against its own round-2 saved snapshot to confirm entry 24 was the ONLY changed command and design_notes/scope/non_goals are otherwise untouched. Both gates confirm: fit for implementation as-is. status flips to 'approved'; scope.files_create/files_modify, non_goals, and estimated_lines are unchanged from revision 2's content.

amendments.target: verification (.agent/ immutability guard entry) glob allowlist
amendments.change: Added '.agent/evidence/audit-*' to the untracked-addition allowlist (alongside the pre-existing '.agent/audits/audit-*'), so the per-audit-id RAW EVIDENCE directory this contract's own scope.files_create explicitly requires (.agent/evidence/<audit-id>/finding-ndebt-*.txt) is not misflagged as an out-of-scope untracked artifact.
amendments.rationale: Discovered empirically running the real implementation's full 27-entry verification sweep: the immutability guard's glob covered '.agent/audits/audit-*' (the audit-id directory itself, holding findings.json/delta.json/report.md) but not '.agent/evidence/audit-*' (the disjoint-prefix raw-evidence convention design_notes.execution_id_2_and_audit_id_conventions establishes), so the four real finding-evidence files this feature's own contract requires were incorrectly reported as 'out-of-scope untracked .agent artifact'. This is exactly the evidence-glob-allowlist lesson contract 043's own amendments catalogued and this contract's authoring instructions said to bake in from the start -- an oversight in this specific glob line, not a design error.
amendments.dry_run_proof: Re-ran the revised immutability-guard command against the real implementation state: with '.agent/evidence/audit-*' added, all four finding-evidence files are now correctly recognized as in-scope and the check reports PASS; reverting the glob (removing the new alternative) reproduces the original four 'out-of-scope untracked .agent artifact' failures, confirming the fix is load-bearing and not a no-op. A genuinely unrelated untracked path (e.g. a stray .agent/evidence/bogus.txt not matching any allowed glob) is still correctly caught, confirming the allowlist remains bounded, not blanket.
